title: Computing SVD using Power Iteration
description: Simplified algorithm to compute largest singular value and vectors
code:
ALGORITHM Power Iteration for SVD
INPUT: Matrix A (m×n), max iterations k
OUTPUT: Largest singular value σ, vectors u, v

BEGIN
 // Initialize random vector
 v ← random unit vector of size n
 
 FOR i = 1 TO k DO
 // Power iteration on A^TA
 u ← normalize(A × v)
 v ← normalize(A^T × u)
 σ ← ||A × v||
 END FOR
 
 RETURN σ, u, v
END
explanation: Power iteration finds dominant singular value by repeatedly multiplying by A and A^T, converging to the largest singular value and corresponding singular vectors.

title: Low-Rank Approximation using SVD
description: Computing rank-k approximation of a matrix
code:
ALGORITHM LowRankApproximation
INPUT: Matrix A, rank k
OUTPUT: Approximated matrix A_k

BEGIN
 // Step 1: Compute SVD
 (U, Σ, V) ← SVD(A)
 
 // Step 2: Keep only top k singular values
 Σ_k ← zero matrix
 FOR i = 1 TO k DO
 Σ_k[i,i] ← Σ[i,i]
 END FOR
 
 U_k ← first k columns of U
 V_k ← first k columns of V
 
 // Step 3: Reconstruct
 A_k ← U_k × Σ_k × V_k^T
 
 RETURN A_k
END
explanation: Uses Eckart-Young theorem to compute optimal rank-k approximation. Only stores k largest singular values, reducing storage from O(mn) to O(k(m+n)).

title: Computing Pseudoinverse using SVD
description: Finding Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse of a matrix
code:
ALGORITHM PseudoinverseSVD
INPUT: Matrix A (m×n)
OUTPUT: Pseudoinverse A⁺ (n×m)

BEGIN
 // Compute SVD
 (U, Σ, V) ← SVD(A)
 
 // Create Σ⁺ (n×m)
 Σ⁺ ← zero matrix of size n×m
 r ← rank(A)
 
 FOR i = 1 TO r DO
 IF Σ[i,i] > 0 THEN
 Σ⁺[i,i] ← 1 / Σ[i,i]
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Compute A⁺ = VΣ⁺U^T
 A⁺ ← V × Σ⁺ × U^T
 
 RETURN A⁺
END
explanation: Computes pseudoinverse which solves least squares problems. Useful when A is singular or rectangular with no exact inverse.
